profile.name: Dean Yang
profile.bio: A Full Stack Engineer transitioning from the medical field to technology. Leveraging communication and problem-solving skills cultivated in the healthcare industry, applying them to software development while continuously learning new technologies. Currently focused on backend technologies and AI applications, documenting learning progress in a personal technical blog.

items.description: Core member of the FBM Restaurant Management System development team. Led the architecture design and development of the cloud reservation system, significantly improving booking processing efficiency by 35%. Designed and implemented database transaction mechanisms to handle inventory deduction and potential overselling in high-concurrency scenarios, ensuring data consistency and system stability under load.

projects.name: FBM Restaurant Management System
projects.description: Built a commercially launched reservation software from scratch. Designed and implemented high-concurrency booking APIs supporting multi-store operations and real-time inventory deduction. Participated in database schema design and query optimization, and introduced caching mechanisms to handle peak traffic, ensuring system stability and data consistency under high load.

projects.description: Assisted in maintaining existing projects, performing feature optimizations and bug fixes.

items.description: Progressed from a junior role to a full stack developer, delivering a comprehensive recycling car tracking system. Developed an end-to-end tracking system using Spring Boot and Android for real-time logistics insights. Instituted streamlined workflows that enhanced team productivity and code quality.
